---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

```{python}
#Data load Part

import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path


BASE_PATH = Path("/Users/yuanyuan/Downloads/final_project/data")

SERIES_FILES = {
    "cpi":      "CPIAUCSL.csv",   #Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers
    "gs10":     "GS10.csv",       #Market Yield on U.S. Treasury Securities at 10-Year Constant Maturity
    "indpro":   "INDPRO.csv",     #Industrial Production
    "tb3ms":    "TB3MS.csv",      #3-Month Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate, Discount Basis
    "unrate":   "UNRATE.csv",     #Unemployment Rate
    "usrec":    "USREC.csv",      #NBER based Recession Indicators for the United States
}


def load_fred_series(file_name: str, new_col_name: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    
    path = BASE_PATH / file_name
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # FRED colname：DATE or observation_date
    if "observation_date" in df.columns:
        date_col = "observation_date"
    elif "DATE" in df.columns:
        date_col = "DATE"
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Cannot find date column in {file_name}")


    value_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c != date_col]
    if len(value_cols) != 1:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected 1 value column in {file_name}, got {value_cols}")
    value_col = value_cols[0]

    # rename date
    df[date_col] = pd.to_datetime(df[date_col])
    df = (
        df
        .rename(columns={date_col: "date", value_col: new_col_name})
        .set_index("date")
        .sort_index()
    )

    # convert to numeric
    df[new_col_name] = pd.to_numeric(df[new_col_name], errors="coerce")

    return df


dfs = []
for col_name, file_name in SERIES_FILES.items():
    tmp = load_fred_series(file_name, col_name)
    dfs.append(tmp)


macro = dfs[0]
for tmp in dfs[1:]:
    macro = macro.join(tmp, how="inner")




print("Start dates:\n", macro.apply(lambda s: s.first_valid_index()))
print("End dates:\n", macro.apply(lambda s: s.last_valid_index()))


start_date = "1953-04-01"   # GS10 start date
end_date   = "2026-01-01"   # all series end date
macro = macro.loc[start_date:end_date]


macro = macro.ffill().bfill()
macro = macro.dropna()

macro["usrec"] = macro["usrec"].astype(int)


print(macro.head())
print(macro.tail())
print(macro.describe().T)

macro.to_csv(BASE_PATH/"macro_merged_clean.csv")

```
